title: International Support Grows as Ebola Outbreak Challenges DRC
subtitle: Global leaders pledge millions to fight a rare strain of the virus as local communities face economic and health struggles.

summary.en: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is currently managing a serious Ebola outbreak involving a rare viral strain. International groups, including the EU and G7, have promised nearly 500 million euros to help with medical support and research. Local communities in Bunia are struggling with both the fear of infection and a sharp decline in their daily income. Experts note that the virus is difficult to treat because existing vaccines are not fully effective against this specific strain. Global health authorities continue to emphasize that international cooperation is essential to stop the spread of the disease.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國目前正處理一場涉及罕見病毒株的嚴重伊波拉疫情。包括歐盟與 G7 在內的國際組織已承諾提供近 5 億歐元，用於醫療支援與研究。布尼亞市的當地社區正同時面臨感染恐懼與日常收入銳減的困境。專家指出，由於現有疫苗對此特定病毒株的效力有限，導致病毒難以治療。全球衛生當局持續強調，國際合作對於阻止疾病擴散至關重要。

paragraphs.en: The international community is increasing its efforts to stop a dangerous Ebola outbreak currently spreading through the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). As of mid-June 2026, the European Commission announced it is providing a support program worth approximately 493 million euros to assist with the crisis. This funding aims to provide immediate medical help, support humanitarian efforts in the Great Lakes region, and improve long-term health systems, according to Deutsche Welle (DW).
paragraphs.zh: 國際社會正加強努力，以遏止目前在剛果民主共和國（Democratic Republic of the Congo, DRC）蔓延的危險伊波拉病毒疫情。根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle, DW）報導，歐盟執委會於 2026 年 6 月中旬宣布，將提供價值約 4 億 9300 萬歐元的支援計畫以協助應對這場危機。這筆資金旨在提供即時醫療援助、支持大湖地區的人道救援工作，並改善長期的醫療衛生體系。

paragraphs.en: This announcement follows a meeting of G7 leaders in France, where officials called for a strong, coordinated global response. The World Health Organization has classified this outbreak as a public health emergency of international concern. The current situation is particularly difficult because the outbreak involves a rare strain of the virus known as the Bundibugyo strain. According to the G7, existing vaccines and treatments are not fully effective against this specific version of the virus, making containment and medical logistics a significant challenge.
paragraphs.zh: 這項宣布是在七大工業國集團（G7）領袖於法國召開會議後發布的，官員們在會中呼籲採取強而有力且協調一致的全球應對措施。世界衛生組織已將此次疫情列為國際關注的公共衛生緊急事件。目前的局勢之所以特別棘手，是因為此次疫情涉及一種名為「本迪布焦」（Bundibugyo）的罕見病毒株。根據 G7 的說法，現有的疫苗與治療方式對此特定病毒株的效力並不完全，這使得疫情控制與醫療後勤工作面臨巨大挑戰。

paragraphs.en: Inside the DRC, the impact on local life is severe. In the city of Bunia, residents are living in fear as the virus spreads. The Guardian reports that the outbreak has forced many people to change their daily routines to avoid infection. For example, school principals have installed hand-washing stations and banned outside food to protect students. Meanwhile, local workers are struggling to earn a living. Many businesses, such as used-clothing shops, have closed because of the high risk of contact, and taxi drivers have seen their earnings drop sharply as movement in the area decreases.
paragraphs.zh: 在剛果民主共和國境內，當地民眾的生活受到嚴重衝擊。在布尼亞市（Bunia），居民因病毒擴散而生活在恐懼之中。《衛報》（The Guardian）報導指出，疫情迫使許多人改變日常生活習慣以避免感染。例如，校長們已設置洗手站並禁止外食，以保護學生安全。與此同時，當地勞工正為生計掙扎。許多商家，如二手衣店，因接觸風險過高而被迫關閉，計程車司機的收入也隨著該地區人員流動減少而大幅下滑。

paragraphs.en: Health experts are also highlighting a sense of global inequality regarding how patients are treated. Deutsche Welle (DW) noted that when a US missionary medic was evacuated from the DRC to Germany for treatment in May, it sparked a conversation about why such advanced resources are not more accessible within Africa. While experimental drugs like MBP-134 exist, experts point out that these treatments require specialized clinical settings, stable storage, and constant monitoring—facilities that are often limited in the outbreak zones. As one expert explained, it takes more than just a drug to save lives; it requires a complete medical system.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生專家也強調了在患者治療方面存在的全球不平等感。德國之聲指出，當一名美國傳教士醫護人員於 5 月從剛果民主共和國撤離至德國接受治療時，引發了關於為何非洲境內無法更普及取得此類先進資源的討論。雖然存在如 MBP-134 等實驗性藥物，但專家指出，這些治療需要專業的臨床環境、穩定的儲存條件以及持續的監測，而這些設施在疫情爆發區往往相當匱乏。正如一位專家所言，拯救生命不僅僅需要藥物，更需要一套完整的醫療系統。

paragraphs.en: Despite these challenges, officials remain committed to the fight. Ursula von der Leyen, the European Commission President, stated that health security is a shared responsibility, noting that countries can better protect their citizens by acting together. Hadja Lahbib, the European Commissioner for crisis management, described the situation as a test of global solidarity, promising that the EU would remain a reliable partner to those on the front lines. These responders, including doctors and nurses, continue to work under extreme pressure to save lives in areas where the virus has caused hundreds of deaths.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨這些挑戰，官員們仍致力於對抗疫情。歐盟執委會主席馮德萊恩（Ursula von der Leyen）表示，衛生安全是共同的責任，並指出各國透過攜手合作，能更有效地保護其公民。歐盟危機管理執委拉比卜（Hadja Lahbib）將此局勢形容為對全球團結的考驗，並承諾歐盟將持續作為前線人員可靠的夥伴。這些包括醫師與護理師在內的應對人員，在病毒已造成數百人死亡的地區，持續承受極大壓力以拯救生命。

paragraphs.en: The situation remains unstable as the virus spreads to new health zones in North Kivu and Ituri provinces. According to reports cited by The Guardian, the outbreak was likely circulating for weeks before it was officially identified in mid-May. With the virus spreading through direct contact with bodily fluids, the risk of further infection remains high. International leaders have urged countries beyond the G7 to contribute resources to help stop this threat, emphasizing that the fight against Ebola is a global necessity. As the world watches, the focus remains on providing the necessary tools to the local teams who are working to keep their communities safe from a disease that has already claimed too many lives.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著病毒擴散至北基伍省（North Kivu）與伊圖里省（Ituri）的新衛生區，局勢依然不穩定。《衛報》引述的報導顯示，該疫情在 5 月中旬正式確認前，可能已傳播數週。由於病毒是透過與體液直接接觸傳播，進一步感染的風險依然很高。國際領袖已敦促 G7 以外的國家貢獻資源以協助阻止此威脅，並強調對抗伊波拉是全球的必要任務。在全世界的關注下，焦點仍放在為當地團隊提供必要工具，讓他們能保護社區免受這種已奪走太多生命的疾病侵害。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A specific variety or type of a virus or bacteria.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（拉緊、過濾），這裡作名詞指病毒類型。文中：The current situation is particularly difficult because the outbreak involves a rare strain of the virus known as the Bundibugyo strain.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make enough money to pay for the things one needs to live.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個固定搭配，容易誤以為是單純的「賺錢」。文中：Meanwhile, local workers are struggling to earn a living.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cause the death of someone.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「聲稱」，但在新聞報導中常指奪走生命。文中：As the world watches, the focus remains on providing the necessary tools to the local teams who are working to keep their communities safe from a disease that has already claimed too many lives.

previousArticles.title: Global Response Grows as Ebola Outbreak Spreads in the DRC
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is facing a serious Ebola outbreak that has already caused hundreds of deaths. International leaders at the G7 summit have pledged significant financial support to help contain the virus. The current outbreak involves a rare strain that is difficult to treat with existing medical tools. Local communities in Bunia are struggling with both the health risks and the economic impact of the disease. Experts warn that global cooperation is essential to manage this public health emergency effectively.

quiz.question: What specific action have school principals in Bunia taken in response to the Ebola outbreak?

options.A: They have closed schools indefinitely to prevent student contact.
options.B: They have implemented hygiene measures and restricted food sources.
options.C: They have requested that the government evacuate students to Germany.
options.D: They have mandated that students wear protective clothing at all times.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Bunia 的校長為了保護學生，安裝了洗手站並禁止外食，這與選項 B 的描述相符。選項 A 錯誤，因為文中僅提到改變日常作息，並未提到無限期停課。選項 C 是關於醫療撤離的討論，與學校管理無關。選項 D 雖然提到防疫，但並未具體提及「強制穿著防護衣」這一點。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might sending experimental drugs like MBP-134 to the DRC be insufficient on its own?

options.A: Because the Bundibugyo strain is resistant to all known experimental drugs.
options.B: Because the local population does not trust modern medical treatments.
options.C: Because the necessary infrastructure to store and administer the drugs is lacking.
options.D: Because the European Commission has refused to fund the distribution of these specific drugs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。第四段提到，專家指出像 MBP-134 這樣的藥物需要特殊的臨床環境、穩定的儲存條件和持續的監測，而這些設施在疫區往往不足。因此，光有藥物是不夠的，還需要完整的醫療系統。選項 A 過度推論，文中僅說現有疫苗效果不佳，未說藥物完全無效。選項 B 文中未提及民眾不信任醫療。選項 D 與文中提到的歐盟提供資金支持的事實相矛盾。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'circulating' most closely mean as it is used in the context of the virus?

options.B: Spreading or existing within a population.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在最後一段中，「the outbreak was likely circulating」是指病毒在被正式識別前，已經在人群中傳播或存在了一段時間。選項 A 是該詞的物理字面意義，但在這裡不適用。選項 C 錯誤，因為文中明確說是在「識別前」就已經在傳播。選項 D 雖然病毒會變異，但「circulating」一詞在此處強調的是傳染範圍與存在狀態，而非變異過程。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: The G7 nations are solely responsible for the failure to contain the Ebola virus.
options.B: The Ebola outbreak in the DRC is a complex crisis requiring global cooperation and systemic health support.
options.C: Experimental drugs are the only effective way to stop the current Ebola outbreak in Africa.
options.D: Economic hardship in the DRC is the primary cause of the rapid spread of the Ebola virus.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章全面探討了疫情帶來的挑戰，包括醫療資源不足、全球不平等、經濟影響以及國際社會的支援，強調這是一個需要全球共同應對的複雜問題。選項 A 過於偏激，文中未指責 G7 為唯一失敗者。選項 C 錯誤，因為文章強調光有藥物是不夠的。選項 D 雖然提到經濟影響，但這只是疫情造成的後果之一，而非病毒擴散的「主要原因」。
